story.description: A clip of conservation, restoration, habitats, reforestation, or protected nature: fishing boat on open sea with seagulls
story.hook: Ocean edges turn wave motion into patterns.
story.lead: Ocean edges turn wave motion into patterns. Watch the wave edge first because water slows, folds, and breaks as the bottom rises. That motion sorts sand and energy across the beach. Which ocean clue should we decode next?
story.script: Ocean edges turn wave motion into patterns. Watch the wave edge first because water slows, folds, and breaks as the bottom rises. That motion sorts sand and energy across the beach. Which ocean clue should we decode next?

story.description: A clip of wild animals in nature: sea lion on pier in sunny waterfront
story.hook: Lions turn hunting into a team problem.
story.lead: Lions turn hunting into a team problem. Watch the spacing before the chase, because lions can use different positions in a group hunt. One animal pressures while another blocks the escape path. The power is not just muscle. Which predator tactic should we break down next?

story.description: A clip of cold-climate animals: seal swimming gracefully in open water
story.hook: Seals can follow wakes with their whiskers
story.lead: Seals steer with their whiskers not their eyes. Watch the left whisker twitch every time it turns. That's because whiskers sense water currents better than eyes can underwater. I thought they were just for touch until now.
story.script: Seals steer with their whiskers not their eyes. Watch the left whisker twitch every time it turns. That's because whiskers sense water currents better than eyes can underwater. I thought they were just for touch until now.
